# minrpc

{{ Version }} {{ License }} {{ Python }} {{ Build }} {{ Coverage }}

Minimalistic RPC utility.

Please do not use. This package is only used within cpymad and pytao.

## Changelog

(dates are in the form `DD.MM.YYYY`)

### 0.1.0

Date: 28.10.2020

- fix UnpicklingError: pickle data was truncated
  see: <https://github.com/hibtc/cpymad/issues/72>

### 0.0.11

Date: 13.04.2019

- remove obsolete OrderedDict export
- simplifications in build/setup scripts
- remove py33 tests

### 0.0.10

Date: 18.10.2018

Pure maintenance release with continuous integration improvements:

- automatically upload release to PyPI
- add automatic style and sanity checks
- fix some style issues
- cleanup in .travis.yml

### 0.0.9

Date: 18.10.2018

- add `__bool__` for `RemoteModule` indicating whether the connection has
  been closed
- return `self` from `ChangeDirectory.__enter__`
- wait for subprocess completion in `Client.close()`

### 0.0.8

Date: 30.08.2018

- improve error prevention during connection shutdown

### 0.0.7

Date: 11.06.2018

- simplify module access, backward incompatible!!
- add overridable `Client._communicate` method

### 0.0.6

Date: 30.11.2017

- improve error checking before/after requests
- catch more exceptions in \_\_del\_\_
- provide copyright notice as unicode

### 0.0.5

Date: 13.11.2017

- allow locking during request (for thread safety)

### 0.0.4

Date: 12.07.2017

- remember error state of connection (whether RemoteProcessCrashed was raised)
- export an OrderedDict type that preserves insertion order

### 0.0.3

Date: 24.09.2016

- fix raised exception type in client

### 0.0.2

Date: 19.09.2016

- fix problem with exception handling

### 0.0.1

Date: 19.09.2016

- copied from cpymad 0.14.3

documentation: Initialize the service with a {class}`Connection` like object.

documentation: Do the full job of preparing and running an RPC service.

documentation: Configure logging module.

methods.name: minrpc.service.Service.run
documentation:
Run the service until terminated by either the client or user.

The service is terminated on user interrupts (Ctrl-C), which might
or might not be desired.

documentation:
Receive and serve one RPC request.

```
returns

True if the service should continue running.
```

documentation:
Dispatch one RPC request.

```
returns

True if the service should continue running.
```

documentation: Execute any static function call in the remote process.

documentation: Close the connection gracefully as initiated by the client.

documentation: Return data to the client.

methods.name: minrpc.service.Service._reply_exception
documentation: Return an exception state to the client.

Base class for a very lightweight synchronous RPC service.

Counterpart to {class}`Client`.

modules.name: minrpc.ipc
documentation: IPC utilities.

functions.name: minrpc.ipc._get_open_file_handles
documentation: Return open file handles as list of ints.

functions.name: minrpc.ipc.get_max_fd
documentation: Return the maximum possible file descriptor or a wild guess.

functions.name: minrpc.ipc.close_all_but
documentation: Close all but the given file descriptors.

functions.name: minrpc.ipc.create_ipc_connection
documentation:
Create a connection that can be used for IPC with a subprocess.

Return (local_connection, remote_recv_handle, remote_send_handle).

functions.name: minrpc.ipc.spawn_subprocess
documentation:
Spawn a subprocess and pass to it two IPC handles.

You can use the keyword arguments to pass further arguments to
Popen, which is useful for example, if you want to redirect STDIO
streams.

Return (ipc_connection, process).

functions.name: minrpc.ipc.prepare_subprocess_ipc
documentation:
Prepare this process for IPC with its parent. Close all the open handles
except for the STDIN/STDOUT/STDERR and the IPC handles. Return a
{class}`Connection` to the parent process.

documentation: IPC connection.

functions.name: minrpc.connection.read
documentation: Read a fixed size buffer from

classes.name: minrpc.connection.Connection

methods.name: minrpc.connection.Connection.__init__
documentation: Create duplex connection from two unidirectional streams.

methods.name: minrpc.connection.Connection.recv
documentation: Receive a pickled message from the remote end.

methods.name: minrpc.connection.Connection.send
documentation: Send a pickled message to the remote end.

methods.name: minrpc.connection.Connection.close
documentation: Close the connection.

methods.name: minrpc.connection.Connection.closed
documentation: Check if the connection is fully closed.

methods.name: minrpc.connection.Connection.from_fd
documentation: Create a connection from two file descriptors.

Pipe-like IPC connection using file objects.

For most purposes this should behave like the connection objects
returned by {func}`multiprocessing.Pipe`.

This class combines two orthogonal functionalities. In general this is
bad practice, meaning the class should be refactored into two classes,
but for our specific purpose this will do it.

- build a bidirectional stream from two unidirectional streams
- build a serialized connection from pure data streams (pickle)

documentation: RPC client utilities.

documentation: Needed for py2 compatibility, otherwise could just use contextlib.ExitStack:

documentation: The MAD-X remote process has already been closed.

documentation: The MAD-X remote process has crashed.

classes.name: minrpc.client.Client

methods.name: minrpc.client.Client.__init__
documentation: Initialize the client with a {class}`Connection` like object.

methods.name: minrpc.client.Client.__del__
documentation: Close the client and the associated connection with it.

methods.name: minrpc.client.Client.spawn_subprocess
documentation:
Create client for a backend service in a subprocess.

You can use the keyword arguments to pass further arguments to
Popen, which is useful for example, if you want to redirect STDIO
streams.

methods.name: minrpc.client.Client.close
documentation: Close the connection gracefully, stop the remote service.

methods.name: minrpc.client.Client.closed
documentation: Check if connection is closed.

methods.name: minrpc.client.Client._request
documentation: Communicate with the remote service synchronously.

methods.name: minrpc.client.Client._communicate
documentation: Transmit one message and wait for the answer.

methods.name: minrpc.client.Client._dispatch
documentation: Dispatch an answer from the remote service.

methods.name: minrpc.client.Client._dispatch_exception
documentation: Dispatch an exception.

methods.name: minrpc.client.Client._dispatch_data
documentation: Dispatch returned data.

methods.name: minrpc.client.Client.get_module
documentation: Get proxy for module in the remote process.

Base class for a very lightweight synchronous RPC client.

Uses a connection that shares the interface with {class}`Connection` to
do synchronous RPC. Synchronous IO means that currently callbacks /
events are impossible.

classes.name: minrpc.client.RemoteModule

methods.name: minrpc.client.RemoteModule.__init__
documentation: Store the client connection.

methods.name: minrpc.client.RemoteModule.__getattr__
documentation: Resolve all attribute accesses as remote function calls.
Wrapper for {mod}`cpymad.libmadx` in a remote process.
